experiment.description: Generate a family of 30 synthetic domains with three group tiers: both-accept (n_pass>=4), clauses-only (n_pass=3, n_exc=1), and incompressible (n_pass<=2). Dream symbolically under clauses-mode and bits-mode; both-accept groups recover under BOTH modes, clauses-only recover under clauses but NOT bits (bits rejects at n_pass=3, n_exc=1 per EX29), incompressible never recover. Test whether bits_saved under bits-mode predicts bits-mode recovery better than clauses_saved predicts clauses-mode recovery. Uses the new-entity generalization protocol (dream full KB, then add new guard-only entities).
experiment.name: does compression predict generalization

group_tiers.both: n_pass>=4, n_exc=0-1 -> clauses AND bits accept
group_tiers.clauses_only: n_pass=3, n_exc=1 -> clauses accepts, bits rejects

params.key_divergence: n_pass=3, n_exc=1: clauses ACCEPTS, bits REJECTS
